mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, equity discount rates decline before earnings respond, so S&P 500 prices should rise over the next month. If the policy rate is flat or rising, the effect should weaken or reverse.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in most falling-FEDFUNDS windows, or if the signal loses predictive power when FEDFUNDS is flat or rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which should lead S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next month. The effect should be strongest when unemployment has risen quickly rather than drifting higher slowly.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average forward return is not below zero after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous upswing in RSAFS and PAYEMS indicates improving consumer demand and labor income, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal requires both sales and payroll growth because either series alone is an incomplete demand check.
failure_condition: Reject if joint RSAFS-PAYEMS improvement does not improve 60-day SPX returns relative to either series alone, or if the interaction term is insignificant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy is too loose and inflation pressure will force tighter financial conditions, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal fails when inflation is stable or falling.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-above-policy episodes are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal disappears when CPI inflation is below the funds rate.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO reflects stronger industrial production and tighter capacity utilization, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest when production is already above its recent moving average.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend INDPRO periods do not produce positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is indistinguishable from random.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST signals cooling housing demand and weaker construction activity, which usually feeds into lower equity risk appetite and softer forward returns for the S&P 500. The effect should show up over a quarter rather than immediately.
failure_condition: Reject if declining HOUST does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign is positive in most housing downturn windows.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is strong while UNRATE is falling, the labor market is adding jobs faster than slack is being created, so consumer spending and earnings expectations should improve and lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The relationship should fail if payroll gains are offset by rising unemployment.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change positive and UNRATE falls
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll gains and unemployment declines do not outperform single-series signals, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in the majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-tightening environment, a slowdown in CPIAUCSL combined with stable or rising RSAFS should support equities by improving real purchasing power without collapsing nominal demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal is not active when inflation is still accelerating.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation falls while RSAFS 3-month growth stays nonnegative
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation-with-stable-sales periods do not lead to positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not superior to CPIAUCSL or RSAFS alone.
